content_en:
As for Umm Aiman, she was a maid who belonged to ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Abdul-Muttalib, and she was from Abyssinia. When the Prophet # was born, and after his father had died, Umm Aiman nursed the Prophet #%. Then, when the Messenger of Allah 2% grew older, he #2 freed her and married her off to Zaid ibn Haarithah 4s. The Prophet #% died first, and then Umm Aiman % died five months later.)

‘Abdullah ibn Ja’far said, “When the Messenger of Allah 2 was born, Haleemah bint Al-Haarith came among a group of

UT Saheeh Bukhaaree, “The Book of Marriage”; chapter, “And Your Mothers From Breast-feeding.”” Hadeeth number: 5101.

women from (the tribe of) Banu Sa’d ibn Bakr, all of whom were looking for infants in Makkah.” It was common during those times for women who lived in the city to give up their infants for a while to women who lived in the desert. Those women were wetnurses; they would rear and breastfeed the infants for a number of months or years, depending on the child. The theory behind this practice was that children who were raised in the desert were at a safe distance from the diseases that commonly afflicted city-dwelling people; furthermore, in the desert, a child would grow up independent and strong, both physically and mentally; and he would learn pure, grammatically correct Arabic, and avoid picking up the slang that was often spoken in the city. Haleemah % herself related the rest of the narration:

“T left among the first group of women, riding a female donkey - which was Qamraa (a colour that is close to either green or white and that contains some mixture of brownness or dullness in it) - that belonged to me. With me was my husband, Al-Haarith ibn ‘Abdul-’Uzzah, one of the children of Sa’d ibn Bakr, who became a part of the Banu Naadirah clan. Our female donkey had become afflicted with bloody wounds (because of the length of the journey). I also brought along an old camel, but by Allah, it would not give forth even a drop of milk. It was a year of drought: people went hungry, to the point that they became very weak and emaciated. Also with me was my son, who, by Allah, did not sleep at night (due to hunger). And I did not even have anything in my hand with which I could distract him. Yet I hoped for rain (and relief). We had sheep, and so we wanted rain. After we arrived in Makkah, the Messenger of Allah # was presented to each one of us, and each one of us disliked (taking) him. We said, “He is an orphan, and it is the father who is kind and generous to the wet nurse. (In the case of this orphan) what can we expect his mother, uncle, or grandfather to do for us?’ Each one of my female companions took an infant (except for me). When I found no other child, I returned to him (the Prophet #) and took him. By Allah, the only reason I took him was that I could find no other infant. I said to my companion (husband), ‘By Allah, I will indeed take this

orphan who is from the children of ‘Abdul-Muttalib; perhaps Allah will benefit us through him, and I will not return with my female companions without having taken anything.’ He said, ‘You are correct (in your decision).’

And so I took him, and returned with him to the place where we had made camp. By Allah, during the very same evening that I brought him with me to the campsite, my breasts gave forth milk, so that I was able to quench his thirst, as well as the thirst of his brother (i.e., her son, and his brother from breast-feeding). His father (again, his father from breast-feeding, and not his blood father) stood up beside the old camel we came with, and he touched it. It was full with milk! And so he milked it. He gave me milk, (and J drank) until my thirst became quenched, and then he drank until his thirst became quenched. He then said, ‘O Haleemah, you do realize, by Allah, that we have taken a blessed soul. Allah has given us through that soul what we could not have hoped for.’ All of us being full, we spent a good and blessed night. Previously, we could not sleep at night with our own child.

Then my companions and I set out for the return journey to our lands. I mounted my Al-Qamraa female donkey, and I carried him (the Prophet #%) with me. By the One Who has the soul of Haleemah in His Hand, I raced past the rest of the caravan; the women exclaimed, ‘Hold on for us! Is this the female donkey that you set out with (at the beginning of our journey)?’ I said, ‘Yes.’ They said, ‘But it had bloody wounds (on its legs) when we arrived, so what happened to it (to cause this change)?” I said, ‘By Allah, I am carrying upon it a blessed boy.’

So we continued our journey, and with the passing of every day Allah % increased us in goodness. When we arrived (home), the lands were afflicted with drought. Our flocks would go out for grazing, and would then return. The sheep of (the) Banu Sa’d (tribe) returned hungry; meanwhile, my sheep returned with their stomachs full and with plenty of milk in them. We would milk (our sheep) and (then) drink. They (the rest of the tribe) would

say, ‘What is it with the sheep of Al-Haarith ibn ‘Abdul-"Uzzah, and the sheep of Haleemah: they come back full and with plenty of milk in them. Woe upon you (they would say to one another)! Go out to pasture where the sheep of their flock go out to pasture.’ They would do so, but as would happen before, their flocks would return hungry; meanwhile, my flock would also return as before (full and with plentiful milk).

And the way in which the Prophet #% grew was very different from the way in which all other children grow: “He grew ina single day the normal growth of an entire year. When he was fully two years old, both his father (i.e., his father from breast-feeding) and I took him to Makkah. We said, ‘By Allah, as long as we are able to keep him, we will never part from him.’ When we met with his mother, we said, ‘By Allah, we have never seen a boy who is more blessed than he is, and we fear upon him the plague and diseases that are rampant in Makkah (Aaminah herself was sick at the time), so let us take him back with us, at least until you are cured from your sickness.’ We continued to persist until she finally gave her permission. We took him back with us and stayed (in our homeland) for 3 or 4 months. While he and his brother (.e., her son, and his brother from breast-feeding) were playing behind the houses (in the area) among a group of small goats and sheep that belonged to us, his brother suddenly (left him and) came racing back to us, and said, ‘Verily, (something has happened to) my brother from the Quraish! Two men, both wearing white garments, came to him, took him, and made him lie down. Then, they split open his stomach.’

His father and I went racing outside, and when we reached him, we found him to be standing up; the colour of his face had completely changed. When he saw us, he raced towards us, crying. Both his father and I embraced him, and tightly held him close to us. (One of us said,) ‘What is the matter with you, may my mother and father be sacrificed for you?’ He said, ‘Two men came to me and made me lie down, they split open my stomach, put something with (or in) it, and then returned it as it was before.’

His father said, ‘By Allah, I am sure that my son has become afflicted (with madness or something similar); let us return to his family, and give him back to them, before something we really fear happens to him.’ And so we took him back to his mother, who, upon seeing us, reproached us for what we were doing and said, ‘What made you bring him back before I asked you to do so? Before, you were indeed bent upon restraining him among yourselves.’ We said, ‘Nothing is the matter. Allah has decreed an end to his breast-feeding, and we are happy with what we see (in terms of his health, etc.).’ She said, ‘Something has happened with you, so tell me about it?’ She did not leave us alone until we told her what had happened. She then said, ‘No! By Allah, Allah would not do that with him. Indeed, my son has a great purpose ahead of him. Shall I not inform you about him? I carried him (in my womb), and by Allah, I never carried a load that was lighter and easier upon me than he was. Then, when I carried him, I was made to see light come out of me, light that illuminated the necks of camels in Basra (or she said, ‘illuminated the castles of Basra’). Then I delivered him and by Allah, he did not come out as other children do; he came out, supporting himself with his hands on the ground; and his head was raised towards the sky.’ She took him, and we departed.”")

Lessons and Benefits from This Story

1) The student of Seerah sees signs and manifestations of the Prophet’s blessedness throughout his life; during the Prophet's infancy, Haleemah % greatly benefited from his blessedness. Once she took the Prophet #¢ into her life, she began to produce a great deal of milk, though previously her

{0 Abu Ya’laa, in Mujma’ Az-Zawanid (8/221), and As-Seerah An-Nabawiyyah, with the Explanation of Al-Khushanee (1/214), by way of Ibn Ishaaq. In the narration of As-Seerah, Ibn Ishaaq explicitly said that he heard the narration (from another narrator). Commenting on this Hadeeeth, Adh-Dhahabee said in As-Seerah An-Nabawiyyah (pg. 8), “This Hadeeth has a good chain, and there are corroborating narrations that strengthen (its grading); therefore, based on those corroborating narrations, the Hadeeth is Hasan (acceptable).””

The Noble Life of the Prophet

breasts did not have enough milk in them for even a single child - her son. Haleemah’s child would cry a lot, thus preventing his mother from sleeping at night, a situation that changed after Haleeman % took custody of the Prophet #. Having plentiful milk to drink, both son and mother began to spend comfortable and relaxed nights. The Prophet’s blessedness also became manifested in the sheep that belonged to Haleemah % and Haarith #, for they began to flow with plentiful milk, though previously their udders would remain dry and empty.
content_id:
Adapun Umm Aiman, ia adalah seorang budak perempuan milik 'Abdullah ibn 'Abdul-Muttalib, berasal dari Habasyah. Ketika Nabi # lahir, setelah ayahnya wafat, Umm Aiman menyusui Nabi #%. Kemudian, ketika Rasulullah 2% tumbuh dewasa, beliau #2 membebaskannya dan menikahkannya dengan Zaid ibn Haarithah 4s. Nabi #% wafat terlebih dahulu, kemudian Umm Aiman % wafat lima bulan kemudian.)

'Abdullah ibn Ja'far berkata, "Ketika Rasulullah 2 lahir, Haleemah binti Al-Haarith datang bersama sekelompok

UT Saheeh Bukhaaree, "Kitab Pernikahan"; bab, "Dan Ibu-ibumu dari Persusuan."" Nomor hadith: 5101.

wanita dari (kabilah) Banu Sa'd ibn Bakr, yang semuanya mencari bayi di Makkah." Hal ini sudah lazim pada masa itu, di mana para wanita yang tinggal di kota menyerahkan bayi mereka untuk sementara waktu kepada wanita yang tinggal di padang pasir. Wanita-wanita tersebut adalah ibu susuan; mereka membesarkan dan menyusui bayi-bayi itu selama beberapa bulan atau tahun, tergantung pada sang anak. Teori di balik praktik ini adalah bahwa anak-anak yang dibesarkan di padang pasir berada pada jarak yang aman dari penyakit yang lazim menimpa penduduk kota; selain itu, di padang pasir, seorang anak akan tumbuh mandiri dan kuat, baik secara fisik maupun mental; ia pun akan belajar bahasa Arab yang murni dan benar secara tata bahasa, serta terhindar dari bahasa gaul yang sering diucapkan di kota. Haleemah % sendiri menceritakan kelanjutan kisah tersebut:

"Aku berangkat bersama kelompok wanita pertama, menunggangi keledai betina — yang disebut Qamraa (suatu warna yang mendekati hijau atau putih dengan campuran cokelat atau kusam di dalamnya) — milikku. Bersamaku ada suamiku, Al-Haarith ibn 'Abdul-'Uzzah, salah seorang dari anak-anak Sa'd ibn Bakr, yang menjadi bagian dari klan Banu Naadirah. Keledai betina kami telah terluka berdarah (akibat panjangnya perjalanan). Aku juga membawa seekor unta tua, namun demi Allah, unta itu tidak mengeluarkan setetes pun susu. Itu adalah tahun kemarau: orang-orang kelaparan, hingga mereka menjadi sangat lemah dan kurus. Bersamaku juga ada anakku, yang demi Allah, tidak tidur sepanjang malam (karena lapar). Dan aku tidak memiliki apa pun di tanganku untuk menghiburnya. Namun aku berharap akan datangnya hujan (dan pertolongan). Kami memiliki domba, dan kami sangat mengharapkan hujan. Setelah kami tiba di Makkah, Rasulullah # diperlihatkan kepada kami satu per satu, dan masing-masing dari kami tidak mau (mengambil) beliau. Kami berkata, 'Beliau adalah seorang yatim, dan ayyahlah yang biasanya berbaik hati dan dermawan kepada ibu susuan. (Dalam hal yatim ini) apa yang bisa kita harapkan dari ibunya, pamannya, atau kakeknya untuk kami?' Setiap satu dari teman-temanku mengambil seorang bayi (kecuali aku). Ketika aku tidak menemukan bayi lain, aku kembali kepadanya (Nabi #) dan mengambilnya. Demi Allah, satu-satunya alasan aku mengambilnya adalah karena aku tidak menemukan bayi lain. Aku berkata kepada temanku (suamiku), 'Demi Allah, aku sungguh akan mengambil anak yatim ini yang berasal dari keturunan 'Abdul-Muttalib; semoga Allah memberikan manfaat kepada kami melalui dirinya, dan aku tidak akan pulang bersama teman-teman wanitaku tanpa membawa apa pun.' Ia berkata, 'Kamu benar (dalam keputusanmu).'

Maka aku mengambilnya, dan pulang bersamanya ke tempat kami berkemah. Demi Allah, pada malam yang sama ketika aku membawanya ke tempat kemah, payudaraku mengeluarkan susu, sehingga aku mampu memuaskan dahaganya, begitu pula dahaga saudaranya (yakni, anakku, dan saudara sepersusuannya). Ayahnya (sekali lagi, ayah sepesusuannya, bukan ayah kandungnya) berdiri di samping unta tua yang kami bawa, dan ia menyentuhnya. Unta itu penuh dengan susu! Maka ia memerasnya. Ia memberiku susu, (dan aku minum) hingga dahagaku terpuaskan, kemudian ia pun minum hingga dahaganya terpuaskan. Ia lalu berkata, 'Wahai Haleemah, demi Allah sungguh kamu menyadari bahwa kita telah mengambil jiwa yang penuh berkah. Allah telah memberi kami melalui jiwa itu apa yang tidak pernah kita harapkan.' Dengan perut yang kenyang, kami melewati malam yang baik dan penuh berkah. Sebelumnya, kami tidak bisa tidur malam bersama anak kami sendiri.

Kemudian aku dan teman-temanku berangkat untuk perjalanan pulang ke tanah kami. Aku menunggangi keledai betina Al-Qamraa milikku, dan aku membawa beliau (Nabi #%) bersamaku. Demi Dzat yang memegang jiwa Haleemah di tangan-Nya, aku memacu lebih cepat dari sisa rombongan; para wanita berseru, 'Tunggulah kami! Apakah ini keledai betina yang sama yang kamu gunakan untuk berangkat (di awal perjalanan kita)?' Aku berkata, 'Ya.' Mereka berkata, 'Tetapi ia memiliki luka berdarah (di kakinya) ketika kami tiba, lalu apa yang terjadi padanya (hingga berubah seperti ini)?' Aku berkata, 'Demi Allah, aku sedang membawa di atasnya seorang anak laki-laki yang penuh berkah.'

Kami pun melanjutkan perjalanan, dan dengan berlalunya setiap hari, Allah % menambah kebaikan kepada kami. Ketika kami tiba (di rumah), tanah-tanah sedang dilanda kemarau. Ternak kami keluar untuk merumput, lalu pulang kembali. Domba-domba (milik) Banu Sa'd (kabilah) pulang dalam keadaan lapar; sementara itu, domba-dombaku pulang dengan perut penuh dan banyak susu di dalam kantungnya. Kami memerasnya (domba-domba kami) dan (kemudian) meminumnya. Mereka (sisa kabilah) berkata, 'Ada apa dengan domba-domba Al-Haarith ibn 'Abdul-'Uzzah dan domba-domba Haleemah: mereka pulang dalam keadaan kenyang dan banyak susu. Celakaan (kata mereka satu sama lain)! Pergilah ke padang rumput tempat kawanan domba mereka pergi merumput.' Mereka pun melakukannya, namun seperti yang terjadi sebelumnya, kawanan mereka pulang dalam keadaan lapar; sementara kawananku juga pulang seperti sebelumnya (kenyang dan banyak susu).

Adapun cara Nabi #% tumbuh sangat berbeda dari cara anak-anak lainnya tumbuh: "Beliau tumbuh dalam satu hari setara pertumbuhan normal satu tahun penuh. Ketika beliau genap berusia dua tahun, ayahnya (yakni, ayah sepesusuannya) dan aku membawanya ke Makkah. Kami berkata, 'Demi Allah, selama kami mampu menjaganya, kami tidak akan pernah berpisah darinya.' Ketika kami bertemu ibunya, kami berkata, 'Demi Allah, kami belum pernah melihat seorang anak laki-laki yang lebih penuh berkah darinya, dan kami khawatir akan penyakit dan wabah yang merajalela di Makkah (Aaminah sendiri sedang sakit saat itu), maka biarkanlah kami membawanya kembali bersama kami, setidaknya sampai kamu sembuh dari sakitmu.' Kami terus memohon hingga akhirnya ia memberikan izinnya. Kami membawanya kembali bersama kami dan tinggal (di tanah air kami) selama 3 atau 4 bulan. Sementara beliau dan saudaranya (yakni, anakku, dan saudara sepersusuannya) bermain di belakang rumah-rumah (di kawasan itu) di antara sekelompok kambing dan domba kecil milik kami, saudaranya tiba-tiba (meninggalkannya dan) datang berlari kencang kepada kami, dan berkata, 'Sungguh, (sesuatu telah terjadi pada) saudaraku dari Quraish! Dua orang, keduanya mengenakan pakaian putih, datang kepadanya, membawanya, dan membuatnya berbaring. Kemudian, mereka membelah perutnya.'

Ayahnya dan aku berlari keluar, dan ketika kami mencapai beliau, kami mendapatinya sedang berdiri; warna wajahnya telah berubah sepenuhnya. Ketika melihat kami, beliau berlari ke arah kami sambil menangis. Ayahnya dan aku memeluknya, dan menggendongnya erat-erat. (Salah satu dari kami berkata,) 'Ada apa denganmu, semoga ayah dan ibuku menjadi tebusan bagimu?' Beliau berkata, 'Dua orang datang kepadaku dan membuatku berbaring, mereka membelah perutku, meletakkan sesuatu padanya (atau di dalamnya), lalu mengembalikannya seperti semula.'

Ayahnya berkata, 'Demi Allah, aku yakin bahwa anakku telah tertimpa (kegilaan atau sesuatu yang serupa); marilah kita kembalikan kepada keluarganya, dan serahkan kembali kepadanya, sebelum sesuatu yang benar-benar kita takutkan terjadi padanya.' Maka kami pun membawanya kembali kepada ibunya, yang begitu melihat kami, menegur kami atas apa yang kami lakukan dan berkata, 'Apa yang membuatmu membawanya kembali sebelum aku memintamu melakukannya? Sebelumnya, kamu sungguh bertekad untuk menahannya di antara kalian.' Kami berkata, 'Tidak ada apa-apa. Allah telah menetapkan berakhirnya masa penyusuannya, dan kami bahagia dengan apa yang kami lihat (dari segi kesehatannya, dsb.).' Ia berkata, 'Telah terjadi sesuatu dengan kalian, maka ceritakanlah kepadaku tentang hal itu?' Ia tidak membiarkan kami pergi hingga kami menceritakan apa yang telah terjadi. Ia kemudian berkata, 'Tidak! Demi Allah, Allah tidak akan melakukan itu kepadanya. Sungguh, anakku memiliki tujuan yang agung di depannya. Bolehkah aku tidak memberitahumu tentang dirinya? Aku mengandungnya (dalam rahimku), dan demi Allah, aku tidak pernah mengandung beban yang lebih ringan dan lebih mudah bagiku darinya. Kemudian, ketika aku mengandungnya, aku diperlihatkan cahaya keluar dariku, cahaya yang menerangi leher-leher unta di Basra (atau ia berkata, 'menerangi istana-istana Basra'). Kemudian aku melahirkannya dan demi Allah, ia tidak keluar seperti anak-anak lainnya; ia keluar dengan menopang dirinya sendiri dengan kedua tangannya di atas tanah; dan kepalanya terangkat ke langit.' Ia mengambilnya, dan kami pun berpisah."")

Pelajaran dan Manfaat dari Kisah Ini

1) Pelajar Seerah melihat tanda-tanda dan manifestasi keberkahan Nabi sepanjang hidupnya; semasa bayi Nabi, Haleemah % sangat banyak mendapat manfaat dari keberkahannya. Begitu ia membawa Nabi #¢ ke dalam kehidupannya, ia mulai menghasilkan banyak air susu, padahal sebelumnya

{0 Abu Ya'laa, dalam Mujma' Az-Zawanid (8/221), dan As-Seerah An-Nabawiyyah, dengan Penjelasan Al-Khushanee (1/214), melalui jalur Ibn Ishaaq. Dalam riwayat As-Seerah, Ibn Ishaaq secara tegas menyatakan bahwa ia mendengar riwayat tersebut (dari perawi lain). Mengomentari Hadeeth ini, Adh-Dhahabee berkata dalam As-Seerah An-Nabawiyyah (hal. 8), "Hadeeth ini memiliki sanad yang baik, dan terdapat riwayat-riwayat penguat yang memperkuat (penilaiannya); oleh karena itu, berdasarkan riwayat-riwayat penguat tersebut, Hadeeth ini adalah Hasan (dapat diterima).""

Kehidupan Mulia Nabi

payudaranya tidak memiliki cukup air susu bahkan untuk satu anak saja - putranya sendiri. Anak Haleemah kerap menangis, sehingga menghalangi ibunya untuk tidur di malam hari, namun keadaan itu berubah setelah Haleemah % mengambil asuhan Nabi #. Dengan tersedianya air susu yang melimpah untuk diminum, baik putranya maupun dirinya sendiri mulai melewati malam-malam yang nyaman dan tenang. Keberkahan Nabi juga tampak nyata pada domba-domba milik Haleemah % dan Haarith #, karena domba-domba tersebut mulai mengalirkan air susu yang melimpah, padahal sebelumnya ambing mereka selalu kering dan kosong.
content_ar:
يصف الجزء الخامس عشر أحداث ما قبيل فتح مكة؛ من عمرة القضاء ورسوخ مكانة الإسلام في الجزيرة، إلى غزوة مؤتة التي كانت أول اصطدام بين المسلمين والبيزنطيين ونموذجًا في الثبات في مواجهة العدو الأكثر عددًا.

ويُبرز الصلابي قيادة خالد بن الوليد في مؤتة وكيف حقّق بها انسحابًا منظّمًا حافظ على الجيش من التدمير، مُقدّمًا ذلك دليلًا على أن العقل الاستراتيجي من صميم فقه الجهاد.
